column1: A pattern formed by molecules undergoing quantum scattering in an experiment at the Vienna Centre of Quantum Science and Technology
The experiment demonstrates that a beam of molecules passing through a fine grating produces patterns consistent with quantum scattering.
A feature of this experiment is that it shows how such patterns form one particle at a time.
The molecular source utilises a laser to evaporate molecules from a thin molecular coating on a window inside a vacuum chamber.
A 3μm collimation slit filters the molecules into a narrow beam.

column2: the particles are incident on a grating with 100nm aperture spacing.
the scattered beam is deposited on a target window where the molecules are illuminated by an infrared laser.

column3: and the resulting pattern is imaged by a CCD camera.
